certificate.moduleName: Palo Alto Networks SD-WAN ION Core Crypto Module
certificate.caveat: Interim Validation. When installed, initialized and configured as specified in section "Secure Operation" of the Security Policy and operated in approved mode
certificate.description: The Palo Alto Networks SD-WAN ION Core Crypto Module is utilized in hardware and software ION form factors. These enable the integration of a diverse set of wide area network (WAN) connection types, improve application performance and visibility, enhance security and compliance, and reduce the overall cost and complexity of your WAN.

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: DRBG: ● SP 800-90Arev1
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.modeMethod: CTR_DRBG (AES-256 bits) Derivation Function Enabled: Yes

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: KAS-SSC ● SP 800-56Arev3
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: KAS-ECC-SSC with P-256, P-384, P-521; key establishment methodology provides between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: KAS ● SP 800-56Arev3
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.modeMethod: KAS (ECC) Scheme: ephemeralUnified: KAS Role: initiator, responder
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: KAS (ECC): Curves: P-256, P-384, P-521; Key establishment methodology provides between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Agreement Scheme per SP800-56Arev3 with key derivation function (SP800-135rev1) Note: The module’s KAS (ECC) implementation is FIPS140-3 IG D.F Scenario 2 (path 2) compliant

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: KTS ● SP800-38F
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: 128, 192, and 256 bits Key establishment methodology provides between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: KTS ● SP800-38F
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: 128, 192, and 256 bits Key establishment methodology provides between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Transport using AES and HMAC

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: SHS ● FIPS 180-4
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Hashing Note: SHA-1 is not used for digital signature generation

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: CKG (SP 800-133rev2)
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: Cryptographic Key Generation; SP 800- 133rev2 and IG D.H.
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Generation Note: The cryptographic module performs Cryptographic Key Generation (CKG) for asymmetric keys as per section 5 in SP800- 133rev2 (vendor affirmed). A seed (i.e., the random value) used in asymmetric key generation is a direct output from SP800-90Arev1 DRBG (DRBG Cert. #A3566).

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: KAS-SSC ● SP 800-56Arev3
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: KAS-ECC-SSC with P-256, P- 384, P-521; Key establishment methodology provides between 128 256 bits of encryption strength

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Agreement Scheme per SP800- 56Arev3 with key derivation function (SP800-135rev1) Note: The module’s KAS (ECC) implementation is FIPS140-3 IG D.F Scenario 2 (path 2) compliant

Key Generation Note: The cryptographic module performs Cryptographic Key Generation (CKG) for asymmetric keys as per section 5 in SP800-133rev2 (vendor affirmed). A seed (i.e., the random value) used in asymmetric key generation is a direct output from SP800-90Arev1 DRBG (DRBG Cert. #A3572).

The module’s entropy source implements Start-up and Continuous health tests defined in SP800-90B, section 4.2. The entropy source utilizes Developer-Defined Alternatives to the Continuous Health Tests which is defined in SP 800-90B section 4.5.
